title: Federal/Provincial/Territorial Ministers Responsible for Seniors Forum - Canada.ca
content:
## Federal/Provincial/Territorial Ministers Responsible for Seniors Forum
Federal/Provincial/Territorial Ministers Responsible for Seniors Forum is an intergovernmental body established to share information, discuss new and emerging issues related to seniors, and work collaboratively on key projects.
- Housing supply needs and challenges for a diverse seniors population in Canada (2025)
- Report on policy options related to the topic of ageism targeting older Canadians (2025)
- The impact of internet connectivity and technological tools on reducing seniors’ social isolation and loneliness during the pandemic and beyond (2025)
More: Reports and Publications

## Meeting of Federal/Provincial/Territorial Ministers Responsible for Seniors
On May 13 to 14, 2026, Federal, Provincial and Territorial (FPT Ministers responsible for Seniors met to discuss collaboration to support Canada’s aging population.
Next meeting Summer 2027

## Provincial and territorial programs
For more senior programs and benefits, visit your provincial and territorial government site.
Choose your location:
- British Columbia (BC)
- Newfoundland and Labrador (NL)
- Northwest Territories (NT)
- Prince Edward Island (PE)
More information on federal programs and services for seniors is available or visit our local Service office. For additional programs, please contact your provincial or territorial government .
1 800 O-Canada (1-800-622-6232)
FPT Seniors Forum Coordination unit mailstop 804 140 Promenade du Portage Phase IV Floor 8 Gatineau, QC  K1A 0J9
